rationale: Single well-capitalized asset (Dangote Refinery), financed against global capital markets and crude-supply arrangements independent of tariff policy. Reached ~85% of nameplate capacity by mid-2025.
34 licensed assemblers, individually under-scaled. Aggregate capacity utilization verified at 29.0%, with a wide spread (6.5% to 70% across firms) showing no consistent scale-up.
Fragmented milling base with porous border enforcement. Rice was Nigeria's single most-smuggled commodity in Q1 2025 despite the tariff wall.
